title: Impeller Speed Deviation — GR-204 Granulation
description: Impeller speed dropped to 580 RPM (validated range: 600–800 RPM) during binder addition phase. Duration: ~4 minutes before self-correction.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Possible motor load fluctuation during high-viscosity binder phase. No equipment malfunction detected. Batch integrity likely maintained based on duration and magnitude.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Review granule moisture content and particle size distribution at sampling point. Consider additional in-process testing. Work order for motor load calibration check recommended as preventive measure.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Perform immediate in-process moisture check (target 8–12%). 2. Increase sampling frequency for this batch. 3. Review motor current logs for trending. 4. Schedule preventive calibration of impeller motor load cell within 30 days.
ai_analysis.regulatory_reference: GMP Annex 15 §6.3 — Process Parameter Deviations; SOP-DEV-001 §4.2

title: Inlet Air Temperature Excursion — 72°C peak
description: Inlet air temperature exceeded validated upper limit of 70°C, reaching 72°C for 6 minutes. Batch B26031201 affected during final drying phase of granulation.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Heating element control loop overshoot. Temperature sensor calibration drift identified as contributing factor.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Full thermal mapping of batch required. Moisture content testing at 5 sampling points. PSD analysis required before batch release.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Immediate: additional moisture and PSD testing. 2. Short-term: recalibrate temperature PID controller. 3. Long-term: implement dual-sensor redundancy for critical thermal parameters.

resolution.rejection_reason: Batch B26031201 rejected. Thermal excursion duration and magnitude outside acceptable limits per QA assessment. Batch quarantined and destroyed.

title: Spray Rate Below Minimum — Binder Distribution Concern
description: Binder spray rate fell to 42 g/min (validated min: 50 g/min) for 8 minutes due to peristaltic pump partial blockage.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Partial blockage of spray nozzle filter detected. CMMS maintenance log shows filter replacement was 45 days overdue.
ai_analysis.recommendation: In-process granule distribution test required. If granule uniformity within spec, batch may proceed with enhanced sampling. CMMS work order for nozzle cleaning and filter replacement required.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Granule uniformity testing at 3 sampling points. 2. Clean and replace spray nozzle filter (CMMS WO). 3. Review PM schedule for spray system — reduce filter replacement interval to 30 days.

resolution.approval_notes: Granule uniformity within spec (CV 4.2%, limit 5%). Batch released with enhanced statistical sampling of final tablets. PM schedule updated.

title: Moisture Content Out of Spec — Post-Drying Check
description: In-process moisture content measured at 4.1% against validated upper limit of 3.5% at the 2-hour drying checkpoint.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Inlet air temperature control confirmed within range. Root cause: initial batch moisture content was elevated (above 15%) entering dryer due to extended granulation time in GR-204. Higher initial moisture requiring extended drying.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Extend drying time by 30 minutes and retest moisture. If passes, batch may proceed. Review BPR for granulation moisture target and drying time correlation.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Extend drying 30 min and retest. 2. Add granulation exit moisture check to BPR as mandatory IPT. 3. Update drying time calculation algorithm to account for inlet moisture.

resolution.approval_notes: Extended drying 30 min. Retest: moisture 2.8% (within 1.0–3.5% limit). Batch released. BPR updated with corrective note.

title: Impeller Speed Deviation — Bearing Wear Detected
description: Impeller speed consistently 15–20 RPM below setpoint for entire batch. Investigation revealed bearing wear causing increased mechanical resistance.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Vibration analysis and CMMS motor current trending confirmed bearing deterioration. PM was 10 days overdue. Increased current draw of 12% above baseline over 3-week period prior to incident.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Immediate equipment quarantine required. Full bearing replacement before return to service. Validation reassessment of granulation parameters post-repair. Batch disposition: reject due to duration of parameter excursion.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Quarantine GR-204. 2. Replace impeller bearings (both primary and secondary). 3. Full IQ/OQ requalification post-repair. 4. Implement vibration monitoring trending. 5. Review PM frequency — reduce to quarterly.

resolution.rejection_reason: Batch rejected. Full-batch parameter deviation for critical process step. Equipment quarantined. Bearing replacement WO issued.

title: Mixing Speed Excursion — MIX-102 Lubrication Blend
description: Mixing speed reached 128 RPM (validated max: 120 RPM) during lubrication blending phase. Duration: ~3 minutes before operator manual correction.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Speed controller setpoint overshoot during lubrication stage acceleration. Lubrication blends are sensitive to over-speed — potential lubricant over-mixing reducing tablet hardness.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Perform blend uniformity test with focus on lubricant distribution. Check tablet hardness profile at compression. If blend uniformity RSD <3%, batch may proceed with enhanced tablet testing.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Blend uniformity test at 3 points (target RSD <3%). 2. Compression hardness profile. 3. Review speed controller ramp rate settings for lubrication step. 4. Add speed ramp rate limit to BPR.

title: Inlet Air Temperature Spike 97°C — DRY-303 Final Drying
description: Inlet air temperature spiked to 97°C (validated max: 90°C) during final drying stage of Omeprazole batch. Duration: 11 minutes. Batch placed on hold. Operator did not respond within 24h — escalated to QA Manager.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Heating element thermocouple failure causing runaway heating loop. CMMS log shows last calibration for DRY-303 temperature sensors was 82 days ago (PM overdue by 22 days).
ai_analysis.recommendation: Batch quarantine required. Full thermal stability assessment for Omeprazole at 97°C exposure. Equipment calibration and PM before restart. Omeprazole is heat-sensitive (deg. temp ~85°C) — batch likely compromised.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Quarantine batch pending stability assessment. 2. Immediate thermocouple calibration and replacement. 3. Run DRY-303 IQ/OQ before resumption. 4. Reduce PM interval to 60 days for temperature-critical processes.

workflow_state.escalation_reason: 24h approval deadline exceeded without operator decision

title: Impeller Speed Borderline Deviation — GR-204 IPQA Review
description: IPQA post-batch review flagged 28-minute period where impeller speed trended at 602–608 RPM (validated minimum: 600 RPM). SCADA did not alarm (value within range). No SCADA data anomaly — borderline detection via BPR trend analysis only.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Borderline speed trending with insufficient deviation magnitude for confident root cause determination. Could be minor motor load variation, sensor drift, or normal operational variability near setpoint.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Granule particle size distribution (PSD) and moisture content testing recommended. Historical cases of similar borderline deviations have not resulted in batch rejection when PSD was within spec.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. PSD analysis at 3 sampling intervals. 2. Moisture content check. 3. Engineer review of motor current data for trending. 4. Consider recalibration of speed sensor.

evidence_citations.resolution: batch released after PSD confirmed within spec

title: Blending Room Temperature Excursion — MIX-102
description: Room temperature in blending area reached 29.5°C (validated operating limit: 28°C) for 22 minutes during pre-compression blending. HVAC system restored temperature within 30 minutes.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: HVAC setpoint drift during scheduled HVAC filter maintenance window. Temperature excursion magnitude (1.5°C over 22 min) is below threshold for Ibuprofen API stability concern (stable to 40°C).
ai_analysis.recommendation: No batch action required based on API stability profile. HVAC maintenance log to be updated. Environmental monitoring data to be appended to BPR.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Append environmental excursion record to BPR. 2. HVAC maintenance procedure to include temperature hold verification before resuming production. 3. No further batch testing required.

resolution.approval_notes: API stability unaffected at 29.5°C. HVAC restored. Environmental data appended to BPR. Batch released.

title: Anomalous Moisture Pattern — DRY-303 No Historical Precedent
description: Moisture content measured at 0.3% (validated min: 1.0%) combined with unusual particulate matter pattern in exhaust air sampling. No similar case found in deviation history. Pattern does not match any known equipment failure mode.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Insufficient evidence to determine root cause. No similar historical cases identified. Unusual particulate pattern not matched in equipment manuals or GMP deviation database.

workflow_state.escalation_reason: Insufficient evidence for AI recommendation — auto-escalated per RAI policy (confidence: 0.31, evidence: none)

title: Chopper Speed Below Minimum — GR-204 Granulation
description: Chopper speed dropped to 950 RPM (validated min: 1000 RPM) for approximately 5 minutes during mid-granulation. Batch continued after self-correction.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Temporary power grid fluctuation causing momentary motor speed reduction. No mechanical fault detected. Short duration and small magnitude suggest no granule de-aggregation impact.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Granule particle size distribution check at next sampling point. If D50 within spec, batch may proceed without restriction.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. PSD check at next scheduled sampling. 2. Review electrical supply stability logs. 3. No equipment action required.

resolution.approval_notes: PSD D50 = 412 µm (target 380–450 µm). Batch released. Power grid event noted in facility log.

title: Product Temperature Excursion 51°C — GR-204
description: Product temperature briefly reached 51°C (validated max: 50°C) for 2 minutes near end of granulation cycle. Second deviation on batch B25120101 — cumulative assessment required.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Residual heat from extended granulation cycle. Cumulative heat load near limit. Combined with INC-2025-0001 (chopper speed dip) suggests slightly extended granulation time causing heat buildup.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Cumulative deviation assessment required (INC-2025-0001 + INC-2025-0002). API thermal stability for Atorvastatin is >80°C — 51°C for 2 min presents no stability risk. Batch release supported.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Document cumulative deviation assessment linking INC-2025-0001 and INC-2025-0002. 2. Review inlet air temperature setpoint near end-of-granulation. 3. Add product temp monitoring at 45-min mark to BPR.

resolution.approval_notes: Cumulative assessment complete. API stability unaffected. Batch B25120101 released with cumulative deviation note in BPR.

title: Mixing Speed Drop Below Minimum — MIX-102
description: Mixing speed fell to 25 RPM (validated min: 30 RPM) for 4 minutes during pre-compression blending of Lisinopril 10mg batch.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Speed controller transient during blending cycle. No mechanical fault detected. Duration insufficient to affect blend homogeneity for Lisinopril formulation at this batch size.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Blend uniformity test (content uniformity by HPLC) at standard sampling points. If CV <5%, batch approved for release.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Content uniformity test. 2. Engineer check of speed controller firmware. No further action anticipated.

resolution.approval_notes: Content uniformity CV 2.8% — within limit (<5%). Batch released.

title: Motor Overload Shutdown — MIX-102 Emergency Stop
description: MIX-102 triggered emergency stop due to motor overload during blending. Motor current reached 148% of rated capacity. Equipment halted mid-blend. Batch in undefined mixing state.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Motor bearing seized post-INC-2025-0003 speed anomaly. Correlation with prior speed instability suggests progressive bearing deterioration. CMMS records confirm MIX-102 bearing replacement overdue by 45 days.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Batch rejection required — blend state undefined at point of emergency stop. Equipment quarantine pending bearing inspection and replacement. Full IQ/OQ before return to service.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Reject batch B25111501 — undefined blend state. 2. Quarantine MIX-102. 3. Replace motor bearings. 4. IQ/OQ requalification. 5. Implement motor current trending in CMMS. 6. Review bearing PM frequency.

resolution.rejection_reason: Batch B25111501 rejected. Undefined blend state due to mid-process emergency stop. Equipment quarantined. Bearing replacement WO issued.

title: Product Temperature 57°C — DRY-303 Post-Granulation Drying
description: Product temperature reached 57°C (validated max: 55°C) for 7 minutes during post-granulation drying. Paracetamol batch. Extended cooling step applied.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Inlet air temperature slightly elevated (68°C) combined with low air flow rate led to heat accumulation in product bed. Paracetamol is stable to 80°C — limited API risk but physical properties (particle size, flowability) may be affected.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Apply 15-minute ambient air cooling cycle. Recheck product temperature. If within range, proceed. Perform bulk density and flowability tests before compression.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. 15-min cooling cycle, recheck temp. 2. Bulk density and flowability tests. 3. Reduce inlet air temp setpoint to 62°C for this product. 4. Update BPR with revised setpoint.

resolution.approval_notes: Cooling cycle applied. Product temp returned to 43°C. Bulk density and flowability within spec. Batch released. BPR updated with inlet temp correction note.

title: Moisture Content 4.2% Out of Spec — DRY-303
description: In-process moisture content test at 2hr checkpoint recorded 4.2% (validated max: 3.5%). Second deviation on same batch following INC-2025-0005. Extended drying required.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: High inlet moisture combined with temperature excursion (INC-2025-0005) created suboptimal drying conditions. Reduced effective drying time due to cooling cycle applied for INC-2025-0005 resolution.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Extend drying 45 minutes with corrected inlet air settings (temp 62°C, flow 1050 m³/h). Retest moisture at 30-minute intervals. If 3 consecutive readings within 1.0–3.5%, batch approved.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Extended drying with interval moisture checks. 2. Add moisture check at 90-min mark to BPR standard protocol. 3. Compute drying time algorithm accounting for inlet granule moisture. 4. Link INC-2025-0005 and INC-2025-0006 in CAPA report.

resolution.approval_notes: Extended drying 45 min. Moisture at 90min: 2.9%. At 120min: 2.6%. Both within spec. Batch released with linked CAPA.

title: Spray Rate Below Minimum — Peristaltic Pump Blockage GR-204
description: Binder spray rate fell to 40 g/min (validated min: 50 g/min) for 14 minutes. Cause: partial blockage of peristaltic pump tubing. Full-batch impact due to extended duration during critical binder distribution phase.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Peristaltic pump tubing blockage confirmed — particulate material in binder solution. Duration (14 min) during binder addition phase poses significant granule non-uniformity risk. Similar event INC-2025-0007 pattern matches INC-2026-0003 with extended duration.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Granule uniformity testing required at 5 sampling points. PSD D10/D50/D90 analysis. If RSD >8% or PSD outside spec, batch rejection recommended per GMP guidelines.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Full granule uniformity panel. 2. Reject if RSD >8%. 3. Clean and replace pump tubing. 4. Add binder solution filtration step. 5. Reduce pump tubing replacement interval.

resolution.rejection_reason: Granule uniformity RSD 11.2% — exceeds 8% rejection limit. Batch B25101501 rejected. Pump tubing replaced and binder solution filtration step implemented.

title: Inlet Air Flow Utility Failure — GR-204 Supply Disruption
description: Inlet air flow dropped to 550 m³/h (validated min: 600 m³/h) for 6 minutes due to compressed air supply pressure drop. Separate event from INC-2025-0007. Both incidents on rejected batch B25101501.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Compressed air main pressure drop affecting inlet air flow. Building utility log confirms pressure event at same time window. This is the second deviation on batch B25101501 — batch already compromised by INC-2025-0007.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Batch already flagged for rejection (INC-2025-0007). Document air flow event as contributing factor in CAPA report. Facility maintenance to investigate compressed air pressure stability.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Document as secondary factor in batch rejection CAPA. 2. CMMS WO for compressed air system inspection. 3. Add low-pressure alarm to building management system with automatic production hold.

resolution.approval_notes: Documented as secondary factor. Batch already rejected per INC-2025-0007. Compressed air maintenance WO issued. Alarm threshold updated in BMS.

title: Room Temperature Critical Excursion — Contamination Risk
description: Blending room temperature reached 30.2°C (limit: 28°C) for 30 minutes. Combined with relative humidity 58% (target <50%). Critical API — Metformin HCl moisture-sensitive. Contamination risk assessment required.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: HVAC system failure: cooling coil fouling causing reduced cooling capacity. Combined temperature and humidity excursion creates moisture absorption risk for Metformin HCl (hygroscopic API). Duration 30 min at 30.2°C and 58% RH exceeds acceptable exposure window.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Batch rejection required. Metformin HCl moisture content likely affected — dissolution and content uniformity risk. HVAC emergency maintenance required before production resumption.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Reject batch B25092501. 2. Emergency HVAC inspection and cooling coil cleaning. 3. Validate room conditions before restart. 4. Add real-time humidity alarm to production monitoring. 5. Implement 15-min HVAC check interval during production.
ai_analysis.regulatory_reference: SOP-DEV-001 §4.5; 21 CFR Part 211.46 — Ventilation, Air Filtration; GMP Annex 1 §4.4

resolution.rejection_reason: Batch B25092501 rejected. Metformin HCl moisture content 1.8% (limit 0.5%) — moisture absorption confirmed during excursion. HVAC emergency repair completed.

title: Documentation Error — Missing Operator Sign-off BPR Page 2
description: IPQA review identified missing operator signature on BPR page 2 (parameter verification at t=0). Required per SOP-QA-001 §3.1. Batch already rejected (INC-2025-0009). Documentation CAPA raised separately.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Operator sign-off skipped during emergency response to environmental alarm (INC-2025-0009). Documentation gap directly linked to emergency response distraction.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Retroactive documentation with explanation of circumstances. Operator retraining on BPR sign-off requirements. Procedure review for emergency-situation documentation protocol.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Retroactive BPR sign-off with QA countersignature and explanation note. 2. Operator retraining (SOP-QA-001). 3. Add emergency-situation documentation checklist to SOP.
ai_analysis.regulatory_reference: SOP-QA-001 §3.1 — BPR Documentation Requirements; 21 CFR Part 211.188

resolution.approval_notes: Retroactive documentation completed with QA countersignature. Root cause linked to INC-2025-0009 emergency response. Operator retraining scheduled.

title: Granulation Time Exceeded — GR-204 Omeprazole
description: Granulation time reached 38 minutes (BPR product specification: 18–28 min). Equipment range allows up to 60 min. Extended mixing due to operator oversight of endpoint criteria.

parameter_excursion.note: BPR product-specific limit; validated equipment range 5–60 min

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Operator did not check granulation endpoint timer. Omeprazole is sensitive to over-granulation (particle size increase, density change). Extended 10 min over spec warrants granule property assessment.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Wet granule density and sieve analysis. If D50 within ±15% of target, batch may proceed. Omeprazole acid-labile — extended mixing may subtly affect enteric coat adhesion.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Granule sieve analysis and density check. 2. Operator retraining on endpoint criteria monitoring. 3. Add endpoint timer alarm to BPR procedure checklist.

resolution.approval_notes: Sieve analysis D50 = 318 µm (target 300–340 µm). Density within spec. Batch released. Operator counselled and retraining scheduled.

title: Chopper Bearing Vibration Anomaly — GR-204 Equipment Quarantine
description: Vibration sensor alarm triggered on GR-204 chopper assembly during granulation. Vibration amplitude 3.2× baseline. Bearing deterioration confirmed by maintenance inspection.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Progressive bearing wear on chopper shaft. CMMS PM record shows bearing last replaced 14 months ago (interval: 12 months overdue by 2 months). Similar pattern to INC-2026-0005 (GR-204 impeller bearing, January 2026).
ai_analysis.recommendation: Batch under GMP review (second deviation INC-2025-0011 + INC-2025-0012). Equipment quarantine required. Bearing replacement and requalification before next use. Batch rejection recommended.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Quarantine GR-204. 2. Chopper bearing replacement. 3. Vibration baseline recalibration. 4. IQ/OQ requalification. 5. Review PM schedule — implement predictive maintenance vibration trending. 6. Review batch disposition considering dual deviations.

resolution.rejection_reason: Batch B25091001 rejected. Dual deviations (granulation time + bearing failure) unacceptable for Omeprazole. Equipment quarantined. Bearing replacement and requalification completed.

title: Compressed Air Failure During Critical Drying — DRY-303
description: Compressed air supply pressure dropped to 1.8 bar (minimum required: 3.0 bar) for 18 minutes during active drying of Atorvastatin batch. Air flow disruption resulted in insufficient drying. Final moisture content OOL.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Compressed air main valve partial failure — pressure drop across facility. 18-minute disruption during active drying insufficient for complete moisture removal. Final moisture content 4.8% (limit 3.5%) confirms inadequate drying.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Batch rejection required. Atorvastatin moisture-sensitive — moisture content 4.8% presents stability and content uniformity risk. Compressed air system emergency repair required before production resumption.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Reject batch B25082801. 2. Emergency compressed air system inspection and valve replacement. 3. Install pressure alarm with production auto-hold at <2.5 bar. 4. Add final moisture content check to batch rejection criteria.

resolution.rejection_reason: Batch B25082801 rejected. Final moisture 4.8% confirmed by QC lab — outside specification (1.0–3.5%). Atorvastatin stability risk. Batch quarantined and destroyed. Compressed air valve replaced.

title: Room Humidity 68% Environmental Excursion — DRY-303 Area
description: Room relative humidity in drying area reached 68% (limit: 60%) during same time window as INC-2025-0013 compressed air failure. Batch already rejected per INC-2025-0013.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Compressed air failure (INC-2025-0013) disrupted HVAC dehumidification circuit — both incidents share the same root cause (compressed air system failure). Humidity excursion is a downstream effect of INC-2025-0013.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Document as secondary contributing factor in INC-2025-0013 CAPA. Dehumidification unit inspection required. No separate batch action needed — batch already rejected.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Link to INC-2025-0013 CAPA. 2. Dehumidification inspection as part of compressed air repair. 3. Add HVAC humidity monitoring to production alarm dashboard.

resolution.approval_notes: Noted as secondary factor linked to INC-2025-0013. No additional batch action. Dehumidification restored after compressed air repair. Environmental monitoring record updated.

title: Impeller Speed Minimum Excursion — GR-204 Brief Event
description: Impeller speed briefly dropped to 585 RPM (validated min: 600 RPM) for 2 minutes during binder addition. Self-corrected. No observable granule impact.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Transient motor load fluctuation during high-viscosity binder addition. Duration (2 min) and magnitude (15 RPM below min) within acceptable minor deviation criteria. No mechanical fault.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Granule moisture check at next standard sampling. If within 8–12%, batch proceeds without restriction. No equipment action required.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Moisture check at next sampling. 2. Log in batch record. 3. No equipment action. 4. Monitor for recurrence over next 3 batches.

resolution.approval_notes: Moisture 9.2% at sampling — within 8–12% target. Batch released. No recurrence in subsequent batches.

title: Spray Rate Above Maximum — Pump Calibration Drift GR-204
description: Binder spray rate reached 215 g/min (validated max: 200 g/min) for 9 minutes. Cause: pump calibration drift resulting in 7.5% over-delivery. Risk of over-wetting and granule agglomeration.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Peristaltic pump calibration drift — last calibration 58 days ago (interval 60 days, borderline). Combined with INC-2025-0015 (same batch), suggests borderline equipment condition. Over-spray of 7.5% for 9 min: potential localized over-wetting.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Granule visual inspection and sieve analysis. PSD D90 — if agglomerates present (D90 >1200 µm), batch reject. If PSD within spec despite double deviation, conditional release with enhanced final tablet testing.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Full granule sieve analysis. 2. Recalibrate spray pump (calibration on next available maintenance slot). 3. Review pump calibration interval — reduce to 45 days for critical parameters. 4. Conditional release if PSD clean.

resolution.approval_notes: PSD D90 = 980 µm (limit <1200 µm). No agglomerates. Dual deviation assessment completed. Batch released with enhanced tablet dissolution testing. Pump recalibrated.

title: Blending Room Temperature 29.2°C — Environmental Excursion
description: Room temperature reached 29.2°C (limit: 28°C) for 18 minutes during summer peak. Paracetamol 500mg blend in progress. Minor excursion.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Seasonal peak outdoor temperature overloading HVAC cooling capacity. Paracetamol API is thermally stable well above 29.2°C. Short excursion, minimal risk.
ai_analysis.recommendation: No batch action required. Document in environmental monitoring log. HVAC capacity review recommended for summer operations.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Document in environmental log. 2. HVAC capacity review. 3. Consider temporary HVAC booster unit during summer months. 4. No batch testing required.

resolution.approval_notes: Environmental excursion logged. API stability unaffected. HVAC review scheduled. Batch released.

title: Mixing Time BPR Specification Exceeded — MIX-102
description: Pre-compression blending reached 42 minutes. BPR specifies 25–35 minutes for this product step. Equipment validation allows 5–60 min. Operator did not notice endpoint timer.

parameter_excursion.note: BPR product-specific limit; validated equipment range 5–60 min

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Operator oversight of BPR timer endpoint. Extended 7 minutes over BPR spec. Over-blending of lubricant (magnesium stearate) is primary risk — lubricant sensitivity can reduce tablet hardness.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Tablet hardness profile at compression. If hardness within 80–120N (target), batch released with note. Lubrication sensitivity assessment.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Tablet hardness profile. 2. Operator retraining on endpoint monitoring. 3. Add audible BPR timer alarm. 4. Log as repeat documentation event (see INC-2025-0011).

resolution.approval_notes: Tablet hardness 96N average (range 88–104N) — within 80–120N spec. Batch released. Audible timer alarm added to blending equipment.

title: Blend Uniformity Failure — RSD 6.8% — MIX-102
description: In-process blend uniformity test result: RSD 6.8% (specification: ≤5% RSD). Third deviation on batch B25073001. Linked to extended blending time (INC-2025-0018). Additional blending cycle performed.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Non-uniform lubrication due to over-blending (INC-2025-0018) causing lubricant coating of API particles. Paradoxical effect: over-blending worsens uniformity by creating lubricant-rich zones. Additional short blending cycle (5 min) at lower speed may redistribute.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Additional 5-minute low-speed blending (40 RPM), retest uniformity. If RSD <5% on retest, batch proceeds. If still OOS, batch rejection required. Consider low-speed re-blend as standard protocol for over-blended batches.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. 5-min 40 RPM re-blend and retest. 2. If pass: conditional release. 3. If fail: reject batch. 4. CAPA: implement endpoint blend uniformity test as mandatory IPT. 5. Link to INC-2025-0018 for root cause.

resolution.approval_notes: 5-min re-blend at 40 RPM. Retest RSD 3.2% (within ≤5% spec). Batch released. Mandatory blend uniformity IPT added to BPR. CAPA closed.

title: Documentation Error — Missing Operator ID BPR Page 4
description: IPQA review of batch B25073001 identified that operator ID was not recorded on BPR page 4 (in-process check section). Required per SOP-QA-001 §3.1. Fourth deviation on this batch.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Operator distracted by multiple simultaneous deviations (INC-2025-0017, 0018, 0019) on the same batch. Documentation failure pattern: highest risk when operators are managing multiple concurrent deviations. Systemic training gap identified.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Retroactive documentation. Systemic retraining on documentation discipline under deviation conditions. Consider peer sign-off requirement when multiple deviations are active.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Retroactive BPR correction with QA countersignature. 2. Trending analysis: documentation errors under concurrent deviation load. 3. Implement peer sign-off checklist when >1 active deviation per batch. 4. SOP-QA-001 revision.
ai_analysis.regulatory_reference: SOP-QA-001 §3.1; 21 CFR Part 211.188 — Batch production records

resolution.approval_notes: Retroactive BPR correction completed. Peer sign-off SOP revision initiated. Training gap CAPA linked to INC-2025-0010 (same issue, different batch). Trending report submitted to QA.
